item.title: No One Knows What to Do About Britain’s Exploding Anti-Semitism
item.description: The first step is admitting that the United Kingdom has a problem.
item.dc:creator: Yair Rosenberg

item.title: Denyce Graves’s Second Act
item.description: Denyce Graves is retiring from performing after a career as one of opera’s leading women. But there’s more work for her to do.
item.dc:creator: Soraya Nadia McDonald

item.title: Music’s Next ‘Disco Sucks’ Moment Is Near
item.description: Do you really like that new song—or is someone manipulating you?
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kornhaber

item.title: The Tragedy of the Tradwife
item.description: Caro Claire Burke on her best-selling novel,Yesteryear, and the retrograde fantasy that inspired it.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Rosin

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: A Streetcar Named What?
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: The Savannah Bananas Bring Back a Negro Leagues Team
item.description: The organization is best known for its dance numbers and trick plays. Now it’s reviving one of the most entertaining—and controversial—franchises in baseball history.
item.dc:creator: Josh Levin

item.title: Trump Shows He’s Still Got Juice
item.description: Yesterday’s election in Indiana sent a message to all Republicans of conscience.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: Kash Patel’s Personalized Bourbon Stash
item.description: The FBI director has been leaving an unusual calling card.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fitzpatrick

item.title: Misoprostol Could Be Next
item.description: If the Supreme Court upholds new restrictions on mifepristone, the other abortion pill may be targeted too.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Tu

item.title: How to Survive Losing a Child
item.description: Danielle Crittenden on losing a daughter, grief, and her new memoir,Dispatches From Grief.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: The Attention-Span Panic
item.description: It’s not just about spans that seem too short.
item.dc:creator: Franklin Schneider

item.title: A Dangerous New Attack on Press Freedom
item.description: According to MS NOW, the FBI has launched an investigation into anAtlanticreporter.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: The FBI Is Reportedly Investigating a Leak to anAtlanticWriter
item.description: Sarah Fitzpatrick reported on concerns about Kash Patel’s drinking and behavior.
item.dc:creator: Matt Viser

item.title: The Perverse Tyranny of a Perfect Transcript
item.description: Why Harvard should cap A grades.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Greene

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: New Colors
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: A Brutal First for the Cruise Industry
item.description: A hantavirus outbreak is serious and unnerving.
item.dc:creator: Katherine J. Wu

item.title: Iran’s Unexpected Resilience
item.description: Two months of fighting have emphasized some of the country’s advantages.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: Yet Another Wasted Met Gala
item.description: The evening squandered an opportunity to really make the case for fashion as art.
item.dc:creator: Shirley Li

item.title: Progressive Activists Are Sometimes on the Wrong Side of History
item.description: Thinking otherwise can enable the left’s worst instincts, as a speech at the University of Michigan’s commencement showed.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Judicial Supremacy Has Arrived
item.description: Last week’s Supreme Court decision didn’t just undermine the Voting Rights Act. It foreclosed the possibility of any new Voting Rights Act in the future, too.
item.dc:creator: Duncan Hosie
